title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Evaluation Brief: Rollback Experiment
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt rollback experiment increment in a production prompt portfolio and release gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.
Monitor outcome, refusal, tool, latency, cost, and drift signals without logging sensitive content unnecessarily.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Rollback Experiment
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Rollback Experiment contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.

content.text: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Rollback Experiment, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt changes are too small to require release controls. This fails the Rollback Experiment contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Rollback Experiment case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Prompt changes are too small to require release controls.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes. Artifact under review: a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Rollback Experiment, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Build Rollback Experiment evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-rollback-experiment-1v4cko" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rollback Experiment into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-rollback-experiment-1v4cko
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Rollback Experiment model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Rollback Experiment applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.
